The Hawks learned of the severity of Horford's injury hours before Thursday's home game against the Bobcats, a blow Josh Smith termed "devastating." Joe Johnson and Smith will be counted on to pick up much of the loss of Horford, on and off the court. Horford, fourth on the team in minutes, averaged 12.4 points, 7.0 rebounds, 2.3 assists, 1.3 blocks and a .553 field-goal percentage (eighth in the NBA) in the first 11 games. He has played both center and power forward, his natural position, in his five...years with the Hawks. -- Who will fill Horford's role and minutes? The team is likely to rely on a combination of players to fill Horford's void, but Josh Smith and Joe Johnson are expected to take on significant roles. Smith, who has been playing power forward, may see an increase in his minutes at center, while Johnson, who can play both guard and forward, will be counted on to provide scoring punch from the perimeter. -- Will the Hawks make a roster move? The Hawks have made several moves this season, including acquiring Mike Bibby and signing Zaza Ponsfeldt. While it's unclear whether they'll make another move, they may consider adding depth at center or power forward to help fill Horford's absence. -- Can the coaching staff keep the team on track for a fifth consecutive playoff appearance, the loss compounded by a compacted NBA schedule? texts.full_text: n be used to execute a piece of code in Unity's main Thread. This is the functions blue print: public static void executeInUpdate(System.Action action) The complete script is really long and will make this post unnecessarily long. You can see the rest of the script helper class here. Then I can use unity's API from another Thread like this: UnityThread.executeInUpdate(() => { transform.Rotate(new Vector3(0f, 90f, 0f)); })Here's a simplified version of your `UnityThread` class using a delegate closure with no memory allocation, meaning it doesn't store the action between frames. This version uses `Action` for the delegate type, which doesn't allocate memory for the lambda itself, but for the captured variables. In your case, as you're not capturing any variables, it doesn't allocate memory for the lambda. ```csharp using System; using UnityEngine; public class UnityThread { private static Action _updateAction; public static void executeInUpdate(Action action) { _updateAction += action; } public static void Update() { _updateAction?.Invoke(); _updateAction = null; } } ``` In this version, `_updateAction` is a public static `Action` that gets invoked in the `Update` method. When you call `executeInUpdate`, the action is added to `_updateAction` using the `+=` operator. texts.full_text: and an optical compensation film (view angle enlarging film or the like), and anti-reflection film. Among various examples, cellulose acylate film as polymer film produced from cellulose acylate is advantageous for high optical property and easy handling in the production. A solution casting process is a typical method of producing cellulose acylate film. In the solution casting, dope is cast on a casting support which is moving, the dope contaiing solvent dissolved with resin such as polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) or polyethylene glycol(PEG). The resultant casted layer passes through an oven to remove residual solvents and then it goes into cooling zone for solidification of film. Finally cellulose acetate sheet can be obtained by cutting a cooled polymer from casting support which is used in solution process, followed stretching the same with tentering machine (Tenter frame) or without using Tenter framing Machine(Free standing). In general case, dope contains resin and solvent at specific ratio to make homogeneous mixture of both. However when temperature change during production occurs by various reasons such as season variation then it is difficult that solution casting process can keep constant quality because viscosity changes with the fluctuation in concentration (i.e., composition) due to vaporization or condensation depending on a kind, amount and molecular weight distribution(MWD). In this case if we try controlling temperature of oven at high level so much as possible than usual condition then it is difficult that solution casting process can keep constant quality because viscosity changes with the fluctuation in concentration (i.e., composition) due to vaporization or condensation depending on a kind, amount and molecular weight distribution(MWD). texts.full_text: the text: To save the output of a script to a file without modifying every puts, use redirection at the command line by adding "> some_file" after the script name. Similarly, using > some_file 2>&1 will route both STDOUT and STDERR to the same file. This won't capture anything typed in at a gets as the code waits for input though, because that won't count as program output. If you don't mind changing your code a little, you can temporarily change the interpreter's idea of what STDOUT is by reassigning it to a file: old_stdout = $stdout File.open('output.txt', 'w') do |fo| $stdout = fo # ---- # your code goes here puts "hello world" # ---- end $stdout =

texts.full_text: es are subject to corrosion which can lead to rust development, weakening, discoloration and failure of the surface. Thus metal substrates are typically treated by a variety of methods to make the surface less reactive and more corrosion resistant. In addition, metal surfaces are often subsequently coated with decorative or additional protective coatings such as resin coatings, primers, paints and other surface treatments. One common mechanism of corrosion occurs galvanically when atmospheric oxygen oxidizes the metal of a metal substrate. the transfer of electrons from the metal to the oxygen at electrodissolution sites on its outer surfaces forms iron ions that migrate through water or electrolyte films present in/on these areas, ultimately combining with hydroxyl anions also contained within such envIronments (as well as any dissolved salts) thus forming soluble ferrous oxide hydrates which precipitate out from solution onto exposed metal surfa

The researchers (Professor Andrew Oswald from the University of Warwick, Associate Professor of Economics Stephen Wu of Hamilton College and Mary C. Daly and Daniel Wilson both from the Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco) believe the key explanation that may explain this counterintuitive link between happiness and suicide rates draws on ideas about the way that human beings rely on relative comparisons between each other. university of Warwick researcher Professor Andrew Oswald said: "Discontented people in a happy place may feel particularly harshly treated by life. Those dark contrasts may in turn increase the risk of suicide. If humans are subject to mood swings, the lows of life may thus be most tolerable in an environment in which other humans are unhappy." Professor Stephen Wu of Hamilton College said: "This result is consistent with other research that shows that people judge their well-being in comparison to others around them. texts.full_text: charged massive long-lived particles. We report on a search for charged massive long-lived particles (CMLLPs), based on 5.2 fb(-1) of integrated luminosity collected with the D0 detector at the Fermilab Tevatron pp collider. We search for events in which one or more particles are reconstructed as muons but have speed and ionization energy loss (dE/dx) inconsistent with muons produced in beam collisions. The reason for that warning message "no compatible pointer casting" you mentioned, even though C allows such casts between different types without generating errors due to its loose type system and implicit conversions. texts.full_text: ge for nursing. In: 14th International Reflective PracticeConference, 3-5 September, 2008, Rotorua, NewZealand . (Unpublished) Preview PDF 304KbAbstract or SummarySensitive researchpresents particular challenges for the qualitative researcher because it involves topics that are stressful and may cause emotional pain for both the participants and researcher (McCosker & Berber, 2001). This paper presents challenges I have experienced in researchingwith registered nurse participants, who like me, had caredfor a dying family member in palliative care. Theresearch was inspired by mycuriosity about whatit was like for other nursesliving within their family and community as a nurseand how they managed the complexities caring for a dying family member in palliativecare. Thesensitive natureoftheresearch was compounded by the personalexperiences of the participants and theresearcher, which introduced a layer of emotional intensity and reflexivity.Throughout the research process, I was confronted with the need for deeppersonal reflection. I had to constantly question my own experiencesand howthey mightinfluence myinteractions with participants and my interpretation of the data.Thisreflexivity was crucial for maintaining the integrity of the research and ensuring that my personal biases did not contaminate thefindings. One of the key challenges was managing the emotional toll of the research. Listening to the stories of participants and their experiences with palliative care was emotionally taxing. It was important for me to develop strategies tocope with these emotionalchallenges to ensure that mywell-being did notcompromise the quality of the research. Anotherchallenge was ensuring that the researchwas conducted ethically. Given the sensitive nature of the topics, it wasimperative to establisha safe andsupportive environment for participants to share their experiences. Thisinvolved obtaining informed consent, ensuring confidentiality, and providing emotional support where necessary. The research also required a nuanced understanding of thepower dynamics at play. texts.full_text: ted, in groups of two to four, all the residues in the three intracellular loops of the IL-8 type A receptor to alanine and analyzed the mutant receptors transiently expressed in 293 cells. Four residues in the second intracellular loop (Y136, L137, I139, V140) and one residue in the third intracellular loop (M241) were shown to be crucial for mediating calcium signaling in response to IL-8. Other residues in the second and third intracellular loops were also found to affect IL-8RA-mediated signaling, but to a lesser extent. These effects were not due to lower expression or low IL-8 binding affinities to the mutated receptors. Mutagenesis of the residues in the first intracellular loop had only weak effects on the mobilization of calcium induced by IL-8. We then used a coimmunoprecipitation protocol with anti-Gi(alpha)2 antibodies to determine the involvement of the two regions defined above in Gi(alpha)2 coupling to IL-8 type A receptors. Whereas the anti-Gi(alpha)2 antibodies coimmunoprecipitated IL-8 receptors in the wild-type cells, this interaction was lost in cells expressing mutated receptors that affected intracellular calcium mobilization. texts.full_text: a typical fluorescence titration curve for C/EBPα (black circles). Linear regression analysis was performed using the equation y = mx + c, where y is the fluorescence intensity, x is the concentration of C/EBPα, m is the slope of the linear regression line and c is the y-intercept. A linear dependence was observed over a concentration range of 0.05-600 ng·mL^-1 of C/EBPα, with a detection limit of 29 pg·mL^-1. This indicates that the method can detect very low concentrations of C/EBPα. The specificity of the method was assessed by analyzing cell lysates, which are complex samples containing a variety of proteins, and the results showed that only C/EBPα interacted with the probe. Furthermore, in-situ studies of adipocyte differentiation demonstrated the potential utility of the method for monitoring the dynamics of C/EBPα expression in cellular environments. texts.full_text: suggests anti-tumor activities of glucosamine-hydrochloride (GS-HCl). In the present study, we investigated anti-proliferative, growth suppressive and/or pro-apoptotic effects of GS-HCl on YD-8 human oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC) cells. Fundamentally, treatment with GS-HCl strongly inhibited proliferation and induced apoptosis in YD-8 cells, as determined by MTS and DNA fragmentation analyses. Of further note, as measured by Western analyses, GS-HCl treatment led to activation of caspase-3, cytosolic accumulation of cytochrome c, down-regulation of Mcl-1 and HIF-1α, up-regulation of GRP78, an indicator of ER stress, and generation of ROS in YD-8 cells. Importantly, results of pharmacological inhibition studies showed that treatment with z-VAD-fmk, a pan-caspase inhibitor, but not with vitamin E, an anti-oxidant strophylin, GRP78 antagonist or ZnCl2 HIF1α prolyl hydroxylases (PHs) inhibitors abrogated the cytotoxicity of GS-HCL in YD-8 cells. Together these results suggest that induction of caspase-dependent apoptosis and down-regulation of Mcl-1, possibly via ER stress-mediated upregulations ROS production or HIF-α are responsible for anti-proliferative/anti-tumor effects of the glucosamine derivatives. question: Does glucosamine cause cancer? texts.full_text: raced with excitement mixed together by sheer terror. I was not sure if this could possibly have been one that my younger self had asked for, but it certainly seemed like a sign from above or perhaps an angel sent as proof of God's existence and power. The figure began to walk towards us in the direction we were heading home. We both froze with fear; however our instincts told me this was not something I should be afraid off. As he approached, his presence became more apparent – it felt like a warm comforting embrace that calmed my nerves and gave an odd sense of peace. The figure spoke to us in Spanish which we understood perfectly despite the loud noise from wind rushing past our ears. "Do you need help?" He asked with kindness. "I can guide your way home." We were both too scared, but also curious about this encounter; so my friend nodded and I whispered a silent prayer of thanks. The figure led us on an unfamiliar path that we had never taken before yet it felt like the right direction to go in. After what seemed hours (but was only minutes) he stopped at his house. We were safe, warm inside with our families waiting for them back home from their night out partying and drinking away. The figure then turned around towards us one last time; but instead of disappearing into thin air as we had expected him to do – the man simply smiled kindly before walking off without saying another word. This encounter has left a lasting impression on me, it was not just an angel or God himself that I saw in front my eyes. It felt like he wanted us both back ho

Because this Nathan Drake isn't the murderous consumer of all things hidden, shiny and — more often than not — dangerous. Sure, he kills lots of thugs, but that's not all he does. Like the Nathan Drake that I've spent time with, he runs, leaps and climbs up structures of ancient origin. This one also collects relics and treasures that are littered throughout various temples and caves.

texts.full_text: ost enough time to allow Nibs to pass her. H.S. made a start, approaching the line at a clip and crossed barely one second after the gun. Disappointment was felt on account of the failure of Courier to appear in the race. Her owner refused to enter her against H.C., although Courier beat H.S. by 18 seconds in a ten-mile race on August 1, having at that time a 10-second handicap. In the August 15 race H.S. was to have been given nine seconds over Courier. The race was three times around a five-mile double triangle course which includes twelve sharp turns. The trophy for this event is a cup presented year by E. R. Thomas, which must be won by the same captain three times before leaving the custody of the club. Snip, owned by H. D. Elliott, was last winner. Bug, this year's winner, although an untried boat, owned by F.
